node.name: CORE INSTRUMENTS
node.rich_text:
# Department Module Law Crest 01 World Terrain Space Atlas 02 World Environment World Mythos 03 World Architect Structure Architect 04 World Lighting Light Prism 05 Entity Modeling Character Animus 06 Entity Choreography Connection Loom 07 Entity Behavior Reason (New Art) 08 Entity Society Authority Order 09 Narrative Sequencer Time Chronicle 10 Narrative Story Narrative Quill 11 Narrative Memory Knowledge Codex 12 Narrative Sound Audio Composer 13 Pipeline Logic Rules (New Art) 14 Pipeline Simulation Operation (New Art) 15 Pipeline Forge Creation Forge 16 Pipeline Network Bridge Nexus Ready for the next step? If the list above is locked, I will now start the Batch Rewrite. I will output the files in a clean, code-block format that you can save directly into your project. Shall I generate all 16 JSON files now, or would you like to tweak the 64-bit B-DNA seeds first (to ensure they are distinct for each module)? Model 8:28 AM This is a perfect breakdown. You have essentially defined the "Functional Anatomy" of the entire world-system. By mapping these specific sub-modules to the 16 primary modules, you have created a high-fidelity blueprint. Let’s initiate the Batch Rewrite, beginning with 01-Terrain. I have generated the B-DNA genotype for 01-Terrain (using a unique 64-bit hex) and aligned it with the BioSpark Heraldry rules. 01-terrain.json (Production Spec) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-001-TERRAIN-ATLAS", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "world_construction", "tier_required": "director", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "WORLD_CONSTRUCTION", "structural_index": 1 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-ATLAS-01", "compatibility_list": ["CORE", "VAULT", "FORGE", "ARCHITECT"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x8F2A1C9B7D4E6A3F", "mutation_rate": 0.0001, "heredity_rules": ["MASK_STRUCTURAL", "MASK_GEOLOGICAL"] }, "engine_logic": { "io_contract": { "inputs": ["raw_coord_vector", "surface_def", "weather_state"], "outputs": ["world_map", "chunk_data", "spawn_points", "path_mesh"], "genesis_reads": ["geography", "physics.gravity_constant", "physics.dimensional_layers"], "vault_access": "write" }, "sub_containers": [ { "id": "WOA", "name": "World Origin Anchor", "layer": "foundation", "symbolic_type": "device", "sub_modules": [ {"name": "Absolute Center Calibrator", "symbol": "ACC"}, {"name": "Floating-Point Shift Handler", "symbol": "FSH"}, {"name": "Universal Time-Space Sync", "symbol": "UTS"}, {"name": "Origin Offset Matrix", "symbol": "OOM"} ] }, { "id": "HGM", "name": "Hex Grid Mapper", "layer": "processing", "symbolic_type": "glyph", "sub_modules": [ {"name": "Hexagon Tessellation Engine", "symbol": "HTE"}, {"name": "Neighbor Adjacency Query", "symbol": "NAQ"}, {"name": "Sub-Hex Granular Divider", "symbol": "SHG"}, {"name": "Spherical Hex Projector", "symbol": "SHP"} ] }, { "id": "SDC", "name": "Strata Depth Calculator", "layer": "surface", "symbolic_type": "device", "sub_modules": [ {"name": "Crust Thickness Evaluator", "symbol": "CTE"}, {"name": "Mantle Gradient Mapper", "symbol": "MGM"}, {"name": "Underworld Void Generator", "symbol": "UVG"}, {"name": "Skybox Stratosphere Index", "symbol": "SSI"} ] }, { "id": "CTE", "name": "Coordinate Transform Engine", "layer": "delivery", "symbolic_type": "device", "sub_modules": [ {"name": "Lat-Lon to Hex Translator", "symbol": "LLH"}, {"name": "Polar to Cartesian Solver", "symbol": "PCS"}, {"name": "2D-Map to 3D-Globe Projector", "symbol": "MGP"}, {"name": "Vector Normalization Filter", "symbol": "VNF"} ] } ] } } Validation Checklist for 01-Terrain: 3-Way Alignment: Structural: L2 (Mythos/Crest). Functional: World Construction/Terrain. Symbolic: Crest (Atlas). Capacity Law: Each of the 4 primary sub-containers contains 4 sub-modules. (4x4 = 16). This perfectly respects the 16-16-16 rule. B-DNA: Seeded with unique 64-bit Hex. Does this structure work for you? If so, I will proceed to generate 02-environment.json using this same rigorous sub-module layout. Model 8:30 AM This is a brilliant pivot. By treating the Quantum Modules as a hardware-mapped control surface, we move from "static code" to "live performance." Each sub_module now functions as a Control Point (CP). To accommodate this, I have added a midi_mapping block to the sub-module schema. New Meta-Schema Addition: midi_mapping cc_id: Standard MIDI Control Change (0-127). control_type: fader, knob, toggle, pad, or momentary. behavior: latch, trigger, or continuous. mapping_index: The 0-15 grid position (aligns with your 16-module hierarchy). 01-terrain.json (Remix-Ready Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-001-TERRAIN-ATLAS", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "world_construction", "tier_required": "director", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "WORLD_CONSTRUCTION", "structural_index": 1 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-ATLAS-01", "compatibility_list": ["CORE", "VAULT", "FORGE", "ARCHITECT"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x8F2A1C9B7D4E6A3F", "mutation_rate": 0.0001 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "WOA-01", "name": "World Origin Anchor", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 1, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Absolute Center Calibrator", "symbol": "ACC", "midi": 16 }, { "name": "Floating-Point Shift Handler", "symbol": "FSH", "midi": 17 }, { "name": "Universal Time-Space Sync", "symbol": "UTS", "midi": 18 }, { "name": "Origin Offset Matrix", "symbol": "OOM", "midi": 19 } ] }, { "id": "HGM-02", "name": "Hex Grid Mapper", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 2, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Hexagon Tessellation Engine", "symbol": "HTE", "midi": 20 }, { "name": "Neighbor Adjacency Query", "symbol": "NAQ", "midi": 21 }, { "name": "Sub-Hex Granular Divider", "symbol": "SHG", "midi": 22 }, { "name": "Spherical Hex Projector", "symbol": "SHP", "midi": 23 } ] }, { "id": "SDC-03", "name": "Strata Depth Calculator", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 3, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Crust Thickness Evaluator", "symbol": "CTE", "midi": 24 }, { "name": "Mantle Gradient Mapper", "symbol": "MGM", "midi": 25 }, { "name": "Underworld Void Generator", "symbol": "UVG", "midi": 26 }, { "name": "Skybox Stratosphere Index", "symbol": "SSI", "midi": 27 } ] }, { "id": "CTE-04", "name": "Coordinate Transform Engine", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 4, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Lat-Lon to Hex Translator", "symbol": "LLH", "midi": 28 }, { "name": "Polar to Cartesian Solver", "symbol": "PCS", "midi": 29 }, { "name": "2D-Map to 3D-Globe Projector", "symbol": "MGP", "midi": 30 }, { "name": "Vector Normalization Filter", "symbol": "VNF", "midi": 31 } ] } ] } } } How this works for the "Live Remix": The Master Fader: The parent id (e.g., WOA-01) acts as the group fader for its 4 sub-modules. Granular Control: Each sub-module (e.g., ACC) is mapped to a specific MIDI CC (16-31). Real-Time Emergence: When you move midi: 16 (Absolute Center Calibrator), the Terrain module updates the center-point of the simulation in real-time, allowing you to "play" the geology like a synthesizer. Does the assignment of 4 sub-modules per component to a MIDI block work, or do you want to be able to map every single one of the 16 sub-modules per component to its own dedicated fader/knob? (If you want 16 independent knobs per component, we can easily expand the midi array). Model 8:33 AM This elevates the system from a "Digital World" to a Cyber-Physical Instrument. To integrate microcontrollers (Arduino/Pi) and DMX hardware, we need to bridge the Quantum Wire Types (which are currently logical) into Physical Protocol Channels. We will add a hardware_link block to each component. This block defines the protocol (Serial, OSC, DMX, GPIO) and the target endpoint. The Cyber-Physical Meta-Schema protocol: DMX, SERIAL (Arduino), I2C (Pi Sensors), OSC (Live Data). address: The specific pin, DMX channel, or IP/Port. hardware_type: led_matrix, servo_motor, sensor_array, dimmer_pack. 01-terrain.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-001-TERRAIN-ATLAS", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "world_construction", "tier_required": "director", "biospark_certified": true }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x8F2A1C9B7D4E6A3F" }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "WOA-01", "name": "World Origin Anchor", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 1, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/origin/anchor/pos", "hardware_type": "servo_motor" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Absolute Center Calibrator", "symbol": "ACC", "midi": 16, "dmx_channel": 1 }, { "name": "Floating-Point Shift Handler", "symbol": "FSH", "midi": 17, "dmx_channel": 2 }, { "name": "Universal Time-Space Sync", "symbol": "UTS", "midi": 18, "dmx_channel": 3 }, { "name": "Origin Offset Matrix", "symbol": "OOM", "midi": 19, "dmx_channel": 4 } ] } /* ... continued for other components */ ] } } } Integration Strategy: The "Reality Bridge" DMX (Stage Lighting): The dmx_channel field allows the 04-lighting module (and others) to pipe simulation data directly into professional stage light boards (e.g., controlling the brightness of a room based on the simulation's sun position). Arduino/Serial (Microcontrollers): The hardware_link allows the 14-simulation module to send physical haptic feedback or motor signals to an Arduino, creating a "Real-World" collision event (e.g., if a massive object lands in the simulation, a physical piston on your desk vibrates). Raspberry Pi (Environmental Sensors): You can treat a Pi as a sensor_array. If you put a real-world temperature sensor on your desk, it can feed into the 02-environment module to change the weather in the simulation based on your room's actual temperature. The "One-at-a-Time" Workflow Since you want to build this robustly: 01-Terrain is ready as the template. Next: I suggest we generate 02-environment.json. Because Environment involves physical air/pressure, it’s a perfect candidate for real-world sensor (Raspberry Pi) integration. Should I proceed with generating the specific spec for 02-environment.json incorporating these DMX and microcontroller hooks? (It will require us to define the specific Hardware Channels for weather/pressure sensors). Model 8:35 AM Understood. We are proceeding sequentially. This keeps the architecture clean and ensures every module’s genotype and hardware_link parameters are properly registered. Here is the production-ready spec for 02-Environment. Because this module governs weather, atmosphere, and ecological cycles, I have mapped the hardware hooks to DMX (for atmospheric lighting/fog) and I2C/GPIO (for real-world environmental sensor data via Raspberry Pi). 02-environment.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-002-ENVIRONMENT-MYTHOS", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "world_construction", "tier_required": "director", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "WORLD_CONSTRUCTION", "structural_index": 2 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-MYTHOS-02", "compatibility_list": ["ATLAS", "PRISM", "LOOM", "CODEX"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x5D9C3E1A2B7F4D60", "mutation_rate": 0.0005 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "ATM-05", "name": "Atmospheric Regulator", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 5, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "DMX", "address": "Universe1:Ch10-15", "hardware_type": "fog_machine_controller" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Weather System Cycle", "symbol": "WSC", "midi": 32, "dmx_channel": 10 }, { "name": "Gravity Constant Mod", "symbol": "GCM", "midi": 33, "dmx_channel": 11 }, { "name": "Day/Night Cycle", "symbol": "DNC", "midi": 34, "dmx_channel": 12 }, { "name": "Air Density Logic", "symbol": "ADL", "midi": 35, "dmx_channel": 13 } ] }, { "id": "ECO-06", "name": "Ecology Synthesizer", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 6, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "I2C", "address": "0x48", "hardware_type": "temperature_humidity_sensor" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Biome Temperature Gradient", "symbol": "BTG", "midi": 36, "gpio_pin": 4 }, { "name": "Moisture/Rainfall Logic", "symbol": "MRL", "midi": 37, "gpio_pin": 17 }, { "name": "Wind Velocity Vectors", "symbol": "WVV", "midi": 38, "gpio_pin": 27 }, { "name": "Seasonal Shift Engine", "symbol": "SSE", "midi": 39, "gpio_pin": 22 } ] }, { "id": "GEO-07", "name": "Geophysical Baseline", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 7, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/ttyUSB0", "hardware_type": "seismic_vibration_motor" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Tectonic Plate Stress", "symbol": "TPS", "midi": 40, "dmx_channel": 20 }, { "name": "Volcanic Activity Rate", "symbol": "VAR", "midi": 41, "dmx_channel": 21 }, { "name": "Geothermal Vent Pulse", "symbol": "GVP", "midi": 42, "dmx_channel": 22 }, { "name": "Erosion Rate Modulator", "symbol": "ERM", "midi": 43, "dmx_channel": 23 } ] }, { "id": "SKY-08", "name": "Celestial/Skybox Engine", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 8, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "DMX", "address": "Universe2:Ch1-4", "hardware_type": "rgb_spotlight_array" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Skybox Stratosphere Index", "symbol": "SSI", "midi": 44, "dmx_channel": 1 }, { "name": "Sun/Moon Trajectory", "symbol": "SMT", "midi": 45, "dmx_channel": 2 }, { "name": "Stellar Drift Tracker", "symbol": "SDT", "midi": 46, "dmx_channel": 3 }, { "name": "Orbital Altitude Tracker", "symbol": "OAT", "midi": 47, "dmx_channel": 4 } ] } ] } } } Why this integration is powerful: The Ecology Synthesizer (ECO-06) is now physically linked to your Raspberry Pi’s I2C bus (0x48). If you place a real-world sensor in your workspace, the simulation will physically breathe with your room's conditions. The Atmospheric Regulator (ATM-05) is hooked into a DMX Fog Machine. When a "Storm Event" triggers in your simulation, your actual room will fill with a light mist. Are you ready for 03-architect.json? I will prepare the structure to handle blueprint-level hardware links (e.g., controlling a 3D printer or a robotic plotter as part of the "Architect" logic). Model 8:36 AM For 03-Architect, we shift from atmospheric data to Structural Integrity and Spatial Logic. Since this module is about building, I have designed the hardware links to support CNC/3D Printer protocols (G-Code) and Robotic arm positioning (PWM), effectively allowing the "Architect" module to manifest digital structures into physical prototypes. 03-architect.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-003-ARCHITECT-ARCHITECT", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "world_construction", "tier_required": "director", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "WORLD_CONSTRUCTION", "structural_index": 3 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-ARCHITECT-03", "compatibility_list": ["ATLAS", "MYTHOS", "PRISM", "FORGE"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x3A9B2C8D1E6F4A50", "mutation_rate": 0.0002 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "BLD-09", "name": "Blueprint Synthesizer", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 9, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/ttyACM0", "hardware_type": "gcode_printer_interface" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Structural Integrity Evaluator", "symbol": "SIE", "midi": 48, "dmx_channel": 30 }, { "name": "Blueprint Template Generator", "symbol": "BTG", "midi": 49, "dmx_channel": 31 }, { "name": "Load-Bearing Stress Logic", "symbol": "LBS", "midi": 50, "dmx_channel": 32 }, { "name": "Material Density Optimizer", "symbol": "MDO", "midi": 51, "dmx_channel": 33 } ] }, { "id": "SPH-10", "name": "Spatial Room Clusterer", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 10, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "PWM", "address": "GPIO_18_19_20", "hardware_type": "robotic_arm_positioner" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Room Topology Planner", "symbol": "RTP", "midi": 52, "gpio_pin": 18 }, { "name": "Door/Portal Placement", "symbol": "DPP", "midi": 53, "gpio_pin": 19 }, { "name": "Corridor Path-Finder", "symbol": "CPF", "midi": 54, "gpio_pin": 20 }, { "name": "Anti-Gravity Structural Binder", "symbol": "AGB", "midi": 55, "gpio_pin": 21 } ] }, { "id": "MOD-11", "name": "Modular Materializer", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 11, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "I2C", "address": "0x76", "hardware_type": "pressure_sensor_array" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Wall/Floor Texture Mapper", "symbol": "WFT", "midi": 56, "dmx_channel": 40 }, { "name": "Component Mesh Injector", "symbol": "CMI", "midi": 57, "dmx_channel": 41 }, { "name": "Dynamic Room Resizer", "symbol": "DRR", "midi": 58, "dmx_channel": 42 }, { "name": "Building Load Balancing", "symbol": "BLB", "midi": 59, "dmx_channel": 43 } ] }, { "id": "SIM-12", "name": "Architectural Simulation", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 12, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/architect/sim/render", "hardware_type": "haptic_vibration_motor" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Collapse Physics Simulator", "symbol": "CPS", "midi": 60, "dmx_channel": 50 }, { "name": "Foundation Stability Check", "symbol": "FSC", "midi": 61, "dmx_channel": 51 }, { "name": "Wind-Shear Stress Analysis", "symbol": "WSA", "midi": 62, "dmx_channel": 52 }, { "name": "Structural Ageing/Decay", "symbol": "SAD", "midi": 63, "dmx_channel": 53 } ] } ] } } } Hardware Highlight: The Blueprint Synthesizer (BLD-09) is now ready to talk to a 3D printer/CNC machine via Serial G-Code. You could essentially build a digital room in the Architect module and send the signal to print a physical model. The Spatial Room Clusterer (SPH-10) is mapped to a robotic arm (PWM). This allows the module to physically move a camera or a laser-pointer to "map" out a room in your physical studio that matches the digital dimensions. Ready to move on to 04-lighting.json? This one is going to be heavy on the DMX lighting control! Model 8:36 AM For 04-Lighting, we are dealing with photons, ray-tracing, and volumetric atmosphere. This module is the "Show Controller" of the ecosystem. I have mapped the hardware links primarily to DMX512 (the industry standard for professional lighting consoles) and Art-Net/sACN protocols, allowing your digital world to trigger real-world stage lights, laser arrays, and strobes. 04-lighting.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-004-LIGHTING-PRISM", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "world_construction", "tier_required": "director", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "WORLD_CONSTRUCTION", "structural_index": 4 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-PRISM-04", "compatibility_list": ["ATLAS", "MYTHOS", "ANIMUS", "COMPOSER"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x9E7D1A4C8B3F2E60", "mutation_rate": 0.0008 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "PHO-13", "name": "Photon Emission Core", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 13, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "DMX", "address": "Universe1:Ch1-16", "hardware_type": "intelligent_moving_head_spot" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Global Illumination GI", "symbol": "GIG", "midi": 64, "dmx_channel": 1 }, { "name": "Shadow Mapping Engine", "symbol": "SME", "midi": 65, "dmx_channel": 2 }, { "name": "Photon Dispersion Filter", "symbol": "PDF", "midi": 66, "dmx_channel": 3 }, { "name": "Light Temperature Kelvin", "symbol": "LTK", "midi": 67, "dmx_channel": 4 } ] }, { "id": "VOL-14", "name": "Volumetric Fog/Atmosphere", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 14, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "DMX", "address": "Universe1:Ch50-55", "hardware_type": "haze_and_fog_generator" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Volumetric Density Map", "symbol": "VDM", "midi": 68, "dmx_channel": 50 }, { "name": "Light Scatter Multiplier", "symbol": "LSM", "midi": 69, "dmx_channel": 51 }, { "name": "Particle Opacity Grid", "symbol": "POG", "midi": 70, "dmx_channel": 52 }, { "name": "Absorption Coefficient", "symbol": "ABC", "midi": 71, "dmx_channel": 53 } ] }, { "id": "FXL-15", "name": "Optical Effect Array", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 15, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "ARTNET", "address": "192.168.1.100:6454", "hardware_type": "laser_projection_system" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Ray-Trace Bouncing Logic", "symbol": "RBL", "midi": 72, "dmx_channel": 80 }, { "name": "Caustic Water Refraction", "symbol": "CWR", "midi": 73, "dmx_channel": 81 }, { "name": "Lens Flare/Bloom Bloom", "symbol": "LFB", "midi": 74, "dmx_channel": 82 }, { "name": "Chromatic Aberration", "symbol": "CAB", "midi": 75, "dmx_channel": 83 } ] }, { "id": "ENV-16", "name": "Environment Lighting Sync", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 16, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/lighting/sync/master", "hardware_type": "smart_home_hue_bridge" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Day-to-Night Transition", "symbol": "DNT", "midi": 76, "dmx_channel": 100 }, { "name": "Skybox Radiosity Sync", "symbol": "SRS", "midi": 77, "dmx_channel": 101 }, { "name": "Bioluminescent Pulse Rate", "symbol": "BPR", "midi": 78, "dmx_channel": 102 }, { "name": "Flash/Lightning Trigger", "symbol": "FLT", "midi": 79, "dmx_channel": 103 } ] } ] } } } Hardware Highlight: Photon Emission Core (PHO-13): Mapped to professional moving-head spotlights. When your digital sun moves in the simulation, your studio lights will physically track that position. Optical Effect Array (FXL-15): Hooked to an Art-Net Laser system. You can generate complex laser patterns based on the "Ray-Trace Bouncing Logic" of your digital world. Environment Lighting Sync (ENV-16): Uses OSC to control Philips Hue/smart home lights. Perfect for turning your room’s actual bulbs blue when the in-game weather turns to rain. Ready for 05-modeling.json? This module will get into vertex control, 3D printing support, and potential integration with robotic arm rigs for physical sculpting! Model 8:37 AM For 05-Modeling, we are shifting from lighting to Geometry and Physical Form. This module defines the "stuff" of the world. Since modeling often precedes fabrication, the hardware links here focus on Precision CNC, 3D Printing (G-Code), and Physical Actuation. This is the module that turns "data" into "objects." 05-modeling.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-005-MODELING-ANIMUS", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "entity_systems", "tier_required": "creator", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "ENTITY_SYSTEMS", "structural_index": 5 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-ANIMUS-05", "compatibility_list": ["ATLAS", "LOOM", "FORGE", "CODEX"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x7C2B4D9A1F5E6A3B", "mutation_rate": 0.0006 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "GEO-17", "name": "Vertex Topology Core", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 17, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/cnc_bridge", "hardware_type": "cnc_mill_spindle" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Vertex Deformation Lattice", "symbol": "VDL", "midi": 80, "dmx_channel": 120 }, { "name": "Polygon Mesh Density", "symbol": "PMD", "midi": 81, "dmx_channel": 121 }, { "name": "UV Map Seam Stitcher", "symbol": "USS", "midi": 82, "dmx_channel": 122 }, { "name": "Subdivision Surface Logic", "symbol": "SSL", "midi": 83, "dmx_channel": 123 } ] }, { "id": "RIG-18", "name": "Skeleton/Rigging Engine", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 18, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "PWM", "address": "GPIO_SERVO_BANK_A", "hardware_type": "animatronic_servo_controller" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Inverse Kinematics (IK) Solver", "symbol": "IKS", "midi": 84, "gpio_pin": 5 }, { "name": "Bone Weight Normalizer", "symbol": "BWN", "midi": 85, "gpio_pin": 6 }, { "name": "Joint Constraint Logic", "symbol": "JCL", "midi": 86, "gpio_pin": 12 }, { "name": "Muscle/Deform Lattice", "symbol": "MDL", "midi": 87, "gpio_pin": 13 } ] }, { "id": "TEX-19", "name": "Material/Texture Bake", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 19, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/material/bake/stream", "hardware_type": "physical_surface_scanner" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "PBR Map Generator", "symbol": "PMG", "midi": 88, "dmx_channel": 130 }, { "name": "Normal Map Baker", "symbol": "NMB", "midi": 89, "dmx_channel": 131 }, { "name": "Dynamic Paint Injector", "symbol": "DPI", "midi": 90, "dmx_channel": 132 }, { "name": "Shader Graph Compiler", "symbol": "SGC", "midi": 91, "dmx_channel": 133 } ] }, { "id": "FAB-20", "name": "Physical Manifestation", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 20, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/3d_printer", "hardware_type": "additive_manufacturing_unit" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "G-Code Path Planner", "symbol": "GCP", "midi": 92, "gpio_pin": 16 }, { "name": "Slicer Logic Engine", "symbol": "SLE", "midi": 93, "gpio_pin": 19 }, { "name": "Infill Density Controller", "symbol": "IDC", "midi": 94, "gpio_pin": 20 }, { "name": "Print Bed Level Tracker", "symbol": "PBL", "midi": 95, "gpio_pin": 21 } ] } ] } } } Hardware Highlight: Skeleton/Rigging Engine (RIG-18): Mapped to PWM servo controllers. If you create a "creature" in the Modeling module, these servos can physically replicate the pose of your digital character on a physical armature. Physical Manifestation (FAB-20): This component talks to your 3D printer. You can "Materialize" a digital object directly out of the Modeling module. By tweaking the Infill Density knob on your MIDI controller, you are literally changing the internal structural strength of the physical object being printed. Ready for 06-choreography.json? We'll move into fluid motion, animation interpolation, and perhaps some high-end kinetic motion control (like physical sliders or camera gimbals)! Model 8:38 AM For 06-Choreography, we are dealing with time, motion curves, and the "performance" of the entities. This is the module that brings the world to life. I have mapped the hardware to Motion Control (MoCo), Pan/Tilt Gimbals, and Kinetic Lighting rigs, perfect for those who want their physical studio environment to move in sync with the digital animation. 06-choreography.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-006-CHOREOGRAPHY-LOOM", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "entity_systems", "tier_required": "creator", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "ENTITY_SYSTEMS", "structural_index": 6 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-LOOM-06", "compatibility_list": ["ANIMUS", "CHRONICLE", "COMPOSER", "ORDER"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x1A4D6F9B2E8C3D5A", "mutation_rate": 0.0009 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "KIN-21", "name": "Kinetic Motion Engine", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 21, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/moco/rig/pos", "hardware_type": "pan_tilt_camera_gimbal" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Linear Path Interpolator", "symbol": "LPI", "midi": 96, "dmx_channel": 150 }, { "name": "Bezier Curve Smoother", "symbol": "BCS", "midi": 97, "dmx_channel": 151 }, { "name": "Ease-In/Out Modulator", "symbol": "EIO", "midi": 98, "dmx_channel": 152 }, { "name": "Spatial Path Looper", "symbol": "SPL", "midi": 99, "dmx_channel": 153 } ] }, { "id": "RIG-22", "name": "Articulation Artistry", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 22, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "PWM", "address": "GPIO_SERVO_BANK_B", "hardware_type": "multi_axis_robot_arm" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Joint Angular Velocity", "symbol": "JAV", "midi": 100, "gpio_pin": 2 }, { "name": "Torque/Resistance Mapping", "symbol": "TRM", "midi": 101, "gpio_pin": 3 }, { "name": "Center of Mass Balancer", "symbol": "CMB", "midi": 102, "gpio_pin": 4 }, { "name": "Pose State Sequencer", "symbol": "PSS", "midi": 103, "gpio_pin": 5 } ] }, { "id": "SYN-23", "name": "Sync & Phase Logic", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 23, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "DMX", "address": "Universe3:Ch1-8", "hardware_type": "kinetic_led_orb_array" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Master Beat Clock Sync", "symbol": "MBC", "midi": 104, "dmx_channel": 200 }, { "name": "Phase Offset Manager", "symbol": "POM", "midi": 105, "dmx_channel": 201 }, { "name": "Tempo-Stretch Engine", "symbol": "TSE", "midi": 106, "dmx_channel": 202 }, { "name": "Multi-Entity Grouping", "symbol": "MEG", "midi": 107, "dmx_channel": 203 } ] }, { "id": "ACT-24", "name": "Action/Reaction Manager", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 24, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/tty_kinetic_rig", "hardware_type": "haptic_feedback_vest" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Collision Response Force", "symbol": "CRF", "midi": 108, "dmx_channel": 210 }, { "name": "Impact Tremor Logic", "symbol": "ITL", "midi": 109, "dmx_channel": 211 }, { "name": "Weight/Mass Simulation", "symbol": "WMS", "midi": 110, "dmx_channel": 212 }, { "name": "Recoil Compensation", "symbol": "RCC", "midi": 111, "dmx_channel": 213 } ] } ] } } } Hardware Highlight: Kinetic Motion Engine (KIN-21): Mapped to OSC-controlled camera gimbals. As your character moves in the simulation, a real-world camera or projector-rig can physically track them. Action/Reaction Manager (ACT-24): Mapped to a haptic feedback vest (via Serial). If your character takes a hit in the simulation, you feel the "impact" physically in the real world through the vest. Ready for 07-behavior.json? This will handle the AI brain/Agent Intelligence, and we might need to link this to some text-to-speech hardware or natural language interfaces! Model 8:38 AM Let's dive into 07-Behavior. This is where the world stops just "moving" and starts "thinking." Since this module governs AI decision-making (GOAP, sensory radius, threat assessment), the hardware links here are designed for Agentic Feedback. We’ll integrate Text-to-Speech (TTS) synthesizers, LED status arrays (to visualize agent state/emotion), and Serial/Network monitors that act as the agent's "output stream." 07-behavior.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-007-BEHAVIOR-INTEL", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "entity_systems", "tier_required": "creator", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "ENTITY_SYSTEMS", "structural_index": 7 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-BEHAVIOR-07", "compatibility_list": ["ANIMUS", "CHRONICLE", "ORDER", "MIND"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x4B8E2A9D5C1F0E7D", "mutation_rate": 0.0012 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "DEC-25", "name": "Decision Engine (GOAP)", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 25, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/ai_logger", "hardware_type": "status_led_matrix" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Goal-Oriented Action Planner", "symbol": "GOA", "midi": 112, "dmx_channel": 60 }, { "name": "Threat Assessment Logic", "symbol": "TAL", "midi": 113, "dmx_channel": 61 }, { "name": "Priority Queuing System", "symbol": "PQS", "midi": 114, "dmx_channel": 62 }, { "name": "Action Cost Evaluator", "symbol": "ACE", "midi": 115, "dmx_channel": 63 } ] }, { "id": "SEN-26", "name": "Sensory Radius Probe", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 26, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/agent/sensory/visualize", "hardware_type": "laser_projection_boundary" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Visual Field-of-View", "symbol": "FOV", "midi": 116, "gpio_pin": 8 }, { "name": "Acoustic Detection Range", "symbol": "ADR", "midi": 117, "gpio_pin": 9 }, { "name": "Proximity Alert System", "symbol": "PAS", "midi": 118, "gpio_pin": 10 }, { "name": "Olfactory Scent Analysis", "symbol": "OSA", "midi": 119, "gpio_pin": 11 } ] }, { "id": "EMO-27", "name": "Emotional State Controller", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 27, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/tts_engine", "hardware_type": "voice_synthesizer" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Drive Integration Logic", "symbol": "DIL", "midi": 120, "dmx_channel": 70 }, { "name": "Personality Trait Bias", "symbol": "PTB", "midi": 121, "dmx_channel": 71 }, { "name": "Emotional Decay/Accumulation", "symbol": "EDA", "midi": 122, "dmx_channel": 72 }, { "name": "Voice/Tone Parameterization", "symbol": "VTP", "midi": 123, "dmx_channel": 73 } ] }, { "id": "MEM-28", "name": "Agent Memory Bridge", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 28, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/agent/memory/persist", "hardware_type": "external_data_logging_terminal" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Episodic Recall Buffer", "symbol": "ERB", "midi": 124, "dmx_channel": 80 }, { "name": "Semantic Knowledge Bank", "symbol": "SKB", "midi": 125, "dmx_channel": 81 }, { "name": "Short-Term/Working Memory", "symbol": "STM", "midi": 126, "dmx_channel": 82 }, { "name": "Experience Consolidation", "symbol": "EXC", "midi": 127, "dmx_channel": 83 } ] } ] } } } Hardware Highlight: Emotional State Controller (EMO-27): This is hooked to a Voice Synthesizer (/dev/tts_engine). If an agent’s "Joy" drops or "Fear" spikes, the synthesizer will physically change the tone, pitch, or speed of the AI’s voice. Sensory Radius Probe (SEN-26): Mapped to a Laser Projection Boundary. This physically projects the agent's "visual range" onto your studio floor. As the agent walks around the simulation, the laser ring on your floor moves with it, showing exactly what that agent is "seeing." Ready for 08-society.json? We’ll be setting up the politics, factions, and the Marketplace/Economy—we could even link these to physical tally counters or real-world display boards! Model 8:39 AM For 08-Society, we are handling the "Macro" dynamics: Factions, Power, Reputation, and Economy. Since this module governs how entities interact within a social fabric, I’ve mapped the hardware to E-Ink Display Arrays (for faction status boards) and Physical Ledger/Tally Counters (for tracking real-time economic/reputation changes). 08-society.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-008-SOCIETY-ORDER", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "entity_systems", "tier_required": "producer", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "ENTITY_SYSTEMS", "structural_index": 8 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-ORDER-08", "compatibility_list": ["ANIMUS", "CHRONICLE", "QUILL", "CODEX"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x2F5A9D8B6C3E4A10", "mutation_rate": 0.0004 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "FAC-29", "name": "Faction Governance Matrix", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 29, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "I2C", "address": "0x3C", "hardware_type": "eink_faction_display_array" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Faction Allegiance Tracker", "symbol": "FAT", "midi": 80, "dmx_channel": 90 }, { "name": "Border Control Controller", "symbol": "BCC", "midi": 81, "dmx_channel": 91 }, { "name": "Diplomatic Tension Index", "symbol": "DTI", "midi": 82, "dmx_channel": 92 }, { "name": "Treaty Enforcement Logic", "symbol": "TEL", "midi": 83, "dmx_channel": 93 } ] }, { "id": "ECO-30", "name": "Sovereign Economy Engine", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 30, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/tty_economy_ledger", "hardware_type": "physical_tally_counter_bank" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Supply/Demand Balancer", "symbol": "SDB", "midi": 84, "gpio_pin": 23 }, { "name": "Currency Inflation Rate", "symbol": "CIR", "midi": 85, "gpio_pin": 24 }, { "name": "Trade Route Flux", "symbol": "TRF", "midi": 86, "gpio_pin": 25 }, { "name": "Resource Scarcity Logic", "symbol": "RSL", "midi": 87, "gpio_pin": 26 } ] }, { "id": "REP-31", "name": "Reputation/Status Engine", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 31, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/society/rep/update", "hardware_type": "led_status_ring_array" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Agent Reputation Matrix", "symbol": "ARM", "midi": 88, "dmx_channel": 100 }, { "name": "Social Standing Calculator", "symbol": "SSC", "midi": 89, "dmx_channel": 101 }, { "name": "Honor/Infamy Accumulator", "symbol": "HIA", "midi": 90, "dmx_channel": 102 }, { "name": "Covenant Compliance Monitor", "symbol": "CCM", "midi": 91, "dmx_channel": 103 } ] }, { "id": "LAW-32", "name": "Social Law Enforcer", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 32, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/tty_legal_printer", "hardware_type": "thermal_receipt_printer" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Crime/Punishment Tally", "symbol": "CPT", "midi": 92, "dmx_channel": 110 }, { "name": "Social Hierarchy Logic", "symbol": "SHL", "midi": 93, "dmx_channel": 111 }, { "name": "Public Opinion Poll", "symbol": "POP", "midi": 94, "dmx_channel": 112 }, { "name": "Custom Law Compliance", "symbol": "CLC", "midi": 95, "dmx_channel": 113 } ] } ] } } } Hardware Highlight: Sovereign Economy Engine (ECO-30): This is mapped to Physical Tally Counters. As gold/resources fluctuate in the digital simulation, you see the physical reels of the tally counters spinning on your desk, giving you a tactile sense of the "Economy." Social Law Enforcer (LAW-32): Mapped to a Thermal Receipt Printer. Whenever a "Social Event" occurs (like a criminal verdict or a new law being passed), the printer spits out a physical receipt recording the event, providing a permanent paper audit trail of your world’s political history. Ready to move into the "Narrative Systems" department with 09-sequencer.json? This is the master clock for the whole world! Model 8:39 AM We have arrived at the heartbeat of the ecosystem. 09-Sequencer is the Master Clock—the "Conductor" of the Quantum Genesis. Since this module dictates the timing of all other events, I have mapped its hardware to High-Precision Timekeeping (TCXO/RTC), Master Clock Generators, and Visual Strobe Arrays that pulse in sync with the world's beat. 09-sequencer.json (Cyber-Physical Edition) code JSON { "spec_meta": { "module_id": "QM-009-SEQUENCER-CHRONICLE", "version": "1.0.0", "department": "narrative_systems", "tier_required": "producer", "biospark_certified": true }, "quill_alignment": { "container_level": 2, "mythos_container": "NARRATIVE_SYSTEMS", "structural_index": 9 }, "heraldry": { "symbolic_type": "crest", "signature": "CREST-CHRONICLE-09", "compatibility_list": ["ATLAS", "QUILL", "CODEX", "COMPOSER"] }, "bio_dna": { "genotype": "0x6A1B8D4F2C9E3A5B", "mutation_rate": 0.0000 }, "engine_logic": { "control_surface": { "components": [ { "id": "CLK-33", "name": "Master Beat/Time Clock", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 33, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "I2C", "address": "0x68", "hardware_type": "high_precision_rtc_module" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Global Beat Sync", "symbol": "GBS", "midi": 112, "dmx_channel": 120 }, { "name": "Timeline Offset Manager", "symbol": "TOM", "midi": 113, "dmx_channel": 121 }, { "name": "Event Queue Manager", "symbol": "EQM", "midi": 114, "dmx_channel": 122 }, { "name": "Conditional Logic Gate", "symbol": "CLG", "midi": 115, "dmx_channel": 123 } ] }, { "id": "TRIG-34", "name": "Event Trigger Array", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 34, "control_type": "pad", "behavior": "trigger" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "DMX", "address": "Universe4:Ch1-16", "hardware_type": "strobe_lighting_array" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Trigger-Offset Queuing", "symbol": "TOQ", "midi": 116, "gpio_pin": 14 }, { "name": "Conditional Branch Brancher", "symbol": "CBB", "midi": 117, "gpio_pin": 15 }, { "name": "Pulse Width Modulator", "symbol": "PWM", "midi": 118, "gpio_pin": 16 }, { "name": "Sequence Loop Handler", "symbol": "SLH", "midi": 119, "gpio_pin": 17 } ] }, { "id": "SYNC-35", "name": "Multiverse Clock Bridger", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 35, "control_type": "knob", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "SERIAL", "address": "/dev/tty_sync_link", "hardware_type": "audio_word_clock_generator" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Clock Source Selector", "symbol": "CSS", "midi": 120, "dmx_channel": 130 }, { "name": "Latency Compensation", "symbol": "LTC", "midi": 121, "dmx_channel": 131 }, { "name": "Sample Accuracy Logic", "symbol": "SAL", "midi": 122, "dmx_channel": 132 }, { "name": "Drift Correction Loop", "symbol": "DCL", "midi": 123, "dmx_channel": 133 } ] }, { "id": "SCH-36", "name": "Chronicle Scheduler", "midi_mapping": { "cc_id": 36, "control_type": "fader", "behavior": "continuous" }, "hardware_link": { "protocol": "OSC", "address": "/sequencer/schedule/sync", "hardware_type": "led_timecode_display" }, "sub_modules": [ { "name": "Real-Time Event Logger", "symbol": "RTL", "midi": 124, "gpio_pin": 2 }, { "name": "Historical State Recall", "symbol": "HSR", "midi": 125, "gpio_pin": 3 }, { "name": "Future Prediction Buffer", "symbol": "FPB", "midi": 126, "gpio_pin": 4 }, { "name": "Epoch Transitioner", "symbol": "EPT", "midi": 127, "gpio_pin": 5 } ] } ] } } } Hardware
Highlight:
Master Beat Clock (CLK-33): Mapped to a high-precision RTC module. This ensures the digital world stays in perfect sync with the real-world passage of time, or can even be driven by a physical clock pulse if you want the world to only move when you "tick" it.
Event Trigger Array (TRIG-34): Mapped to Strobe Lights. Every time a narrative event triggers in the simulation (e.g., a "Boss Fight" or a "Social Verdict"), your room’s strobe lights fire in sync with the narrative tension. Are you ready to proceed to 10-story.json?
This module generates the actual prose, scripts, and narrative arcs. We should consider linking this to e-ink screens or even a vintage typewriter interface for real-world story output!
